explanation.hook: A ball thrown at an angle follows a curved path shaped by gravity and initial velocity.
keyIdeas: Angle and speed set the trajectory
explanation.analogy: Throwing a paper airplane — aim higher for more airtime, faster for more distance.

questions.question: Which force primarily shapes a projectile's path?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls the projectile downward continuously.

questions.question: At 45° (no air resistance), what is maximized?
questions.explanation: 45° gives the best balance of horizontal and vertical components.

questions.question: What happens to horizontal velocity (ideal case)?
questions.explanation: With no air resistance, horizontal velocity is unchanged.

challenge.title: Peak height
challenge.description: At 25 m/s and 45°, roughly how many seconds until the ball lands? (nearest whole number)
